exampleSentencesTranslated: You carry the box up to the room.
You put the book up on the table.
notes_md:
**lên** means *up* or *to go up*. It shows upward movement or moving to a higher position/place.

As a verb, it often follows another verb to show direction: movement to a higher place, onto something, or northward on a map. It can also mean “increase” (price, level, volume) or “get on” a vehicle.


Opposite idea: **xuống** (down) — very useful pair to remember.

translation.en: We will go up the mountain tomorrow morning.

content.sentence: Chúng ta sẽ ___ núi vào sáng mai.

sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + đi (go) + lên (up) + cầu thang (the stairs)

content.sentence: Chúng ta sẽ ___ núi vào sáng mai.

translation.en: We will go up the mountain tomorrow morning.

example.vi: Tôi sẽ lên núi vào cuối tuần này.
example.en: I will go up the mountain this weekend.
example.es: Voy a subir la montaña este fin de semana.

notes:
lên means up or to go up. It shows upward movement or moving to a higher position/place.

As a verb, it often follows another verb to show direction: movement to a higher place, onto something, or northward on a map. It can also mean “increase” (price, level, volume) or “get on” a vehicle.

Opposite idea: xuống (down) — very useful pair to remember.
